nav.siteTagline: Value investing — trust the power of long-term compounding. Backtest history, understand the future.

backtest.irrTooltip: Money-weighted annualized return: cash flows are computed at each contribution's actual trade date, with final portfolio value as the terminal recovery. More appropriate for DCA backtests than a simple CAGR.

etf.tooltipTrueError: 30-Day True Error: Average difference between daily NAV return and benchmark index return (dividends reinvested) over the last 30 common trading days.
etf.tooltipReturnDiff: 30-Day Return Diff/10K: If you bought ¥10,000 of this ETF 30 days ago, how much more or less you'd have vs the benchmark (dividends reinvested). Positive = ETF outperformed.
etf.tooltipEstError: Estimation Error: Difference between T-2 NAV return and T-2 benchmark index return (dividends reinvested). Reflects tracking deviation.

qdii.chartTitle: 📋 S&P & Nasdaq Fund Tracker (OTC) — Alipay OTC QDII Candidate List
qdii.fetchFailed: Failed to fetch QDII fund data:
qdii.mostlyCClass: Mostly Class C; check service fee
qdii.noMatchingFunds: No funds match the filter criteria
qdii.guideToday1: Start with "Publicly Available", then sort by daily limit high to low.
qdii.guideToday1Alt: No publicly available items via API; you will likely need to wait for quota on Alipay too.
qdii.guideToday2Alt: When the limit field is missing, refer to the Alipay order page.
qdii.guideToday3: Search by fund code to avoid mixing in listed ETFs or sector indices.
qdii.guideShareCode1: Class A typically charges a subscription fee at purchase and does not deduct a service fee from fund assets. Summary: Class A is better for long-term holding.
qdii.guideShareCode2: Class C typically has no subscription fee but charges a daily service fee. The longer you hold, the more you should compare total costs. Summary: Class C is better for short-term holding.
qdii.guideShareCode3: Letters like B/D/E/I have no universal market meaning; they may indicate back-end fees, specific channels, currency classes, thresholds, or institutional shares.
qdii.guideReminder1: QDII limits change frequently; this table serves as a daily snapshot.
qdii.guideReminder2: Final availability, coupons, and account limits are subject to the Alipay app.
qdii.guideReminder3: Class A low-fee candidates: {{list}}. Ultimately, consider service fees, redemption fees, and holding period.
qdii.guideReminder3Alt: Prefer C for short-term, A for long-term is just a rule of thumb. Ultimately, consider service fees, redemption fees, and holding period.
qdii.guideActiveQdii1: First layer by type: equity and balanced funds are equity-oriented; pure bond and balanced bond funds tend toward USD bonds or overseas bonds.
qdii.guideActiveQdii2: Most common types in current sample: {{list}}.
qdii.guideActiveQdii2Alt: When the type field is missing, check the fund detail page for its investment scope.
qdii.guideActiveQdii3: Currently available with a higher limit: {{code}} {{company}}, {{limit}}.
qdii.guideActiveQdii3Alt: No publicly available items via API; you will likely need to wait for quota on Alipay too.
qdii.guideActiveKey1: Don't just look at short-term returns. Focus on the fund manager, thematic scope, regional exposure, scale, and drawdowns.
qdii.guideActiveKey2Alt: For long-term holding, compare Class A subscription fees and total holding costs.
qdii.guideActiveKey3: Zero subscription fee for Class C does not mean low cost. Also check service fees and redemption fees.
qdii.guideDataScope1: This list automatically excludes index funds, ETFs, feeder funds, LOFs, FOFs, commodities, and USD/HKD share classes.
qdii.guideDataScope2: This is a public API screening result and may differ from the full list currently available on Alipay.
qdii.guideDataScope3: For a strict "active equity fund" filter, you can further exclude bond-type QDII funds.
qdii.introNote: QDII stands for Qualified Domestic Institutional Investor. These funds let qualified domestic fund companies invest in overseas markets and are often affected by FX quota, overseas trading calendars, and exchange rates.

vix.tipExtremeCalm: Let profits run; take partial profits. Avoid chasing highs.
vix.tipLowVol: Light positions on the right side; wait on the left. Avoid chasing.
vix.tipNormal: Mean reversion — stick to your plan. Stay the course with DCA.
vix.tipFear: Market pullback — deploy reserves and buy in tranches.
vix.tipHighFear: Likely near a stage bottom — accumulate aggressively and increase DCA.
vix.tipExtremePanic: Historic buying opportunity! Buy aggressively but keep some powder dry.
vix.corrWeakening: SPY/VIX negative correlation weakening — watch for anomaly risk
vix.corrNormal: SPY/VIX still maintaining typical negative correlation
vix.ruleTip:
VIX Zones & Guidelines:
<12  Extreme Calm  ❌ Avoid chasing (let profits run / take partial profits)
12-15 Low Vol       ❌ Avoid chasing (light positions / wait on left)
15-20 Normal Range  ✅ Stay the course (mean reversion / stick to plan)
20-25 Fear Rising   ✅ Buy in tranches (market pullback / deploy reserves)
25-35 High Fear     ✅ Increase DCA (stage bottom / accumulate aggressively)
>=35 Extreme Panic  ✅ Aggressive buy (historic opportunity / keep powder dry)
vix.adviceVixTooltip: VIX is the CBOE Volatility Index, often called the Fear Index. It reflects the market's expectation of ~30-day volatility for the S&P 500. Higher values generally indicate greater market fear.
vix.adviceZoneTooltip: Based on the absolute VIX level: <12 Extreme Calm, 12-15 Low Volatility, 15-20 Normal Range, 20-25 Fear Rising, 25-30 High Fear, >30 Extreme Panic.
vix.advicePercentileTooltip: The historical position of the current VIX value among the past ~1 year of trading days. E.g., 79% means VIX was at or below the current level on ~79% of days — higher values = closer to 1-year highs.
vix.adviceCorrelationTooltip: The correlation coefficient between SPY daily returns and VIX daily returns over the last 30 trading days. Typically negative (SPY down → VIX up). Closer to -1 = stronger negative correlation; near 0 = weakening; positive = structural anomaly or risk signal.
vix.adviceTipTooltip: A qualitative note based on current VIX absolute level, 1-year percentile, and SPY/VIX 30-day correlation. For risk observation only — not investment advice.

seo.indexTitle: GlobalAssetHistory — Historical Returns & DCA Backtest Tool
seo.indexDescription: Explore historical yearly, monthly, and daily returns for US stocks, ETFs, crypto, and A-share indices. Run DCA backtests with daily data.
seo.backtestDescription: Backtest DCA strategies (lump sum, daily, weekly, monthly) using historical daily price data.
seo.detailTitle: Return Detail: Yearly and Monthly Heatmap — GlobalAssetHistory
seo.detailDescription: View yearly and monthly returns, average, median, and win rate for a single stock, ETF, or crypto asset.
seo.crashDescription: Analyze historical crash events for US stocks: drawdown magnitude, recovery days, and bottoming process.
seo.etfDescription: Real-time tracking of A-share listed S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 ETFs: premiums, estimation errors, and return analysis.
seo.qdiiDescription: Track OTC QDII fund availability, fee structures, purchase limits, and true tracking errors.
seo.vixDescription: VIX overlay with SPY/QQQ, historical panic zone mapping, and market sentiment reference.
seo.knowledgeDescription: US stock investing guides, ETF basics, macro data interpretation, and financial terminology reference.
seo.knowledgeHowToBuyTitle: How to Buy US Stocks and ETFs with Stablecoins — GlobalAssetHistory
seo.knowledgeHowToBuyDescription: A practical guide to buying US stocks and ETFs with USDT or USDC through crypto platforms, including product structure, suitability, and risks.
seo.knowledgeEtfIntroTitle: Core US ETF Guide: SPY, VOO, QQQ, SMH, DRAM, EWY — GlobalAssetHistory
seo.knowledgeEtfIntroDescription: Compare core US ETFs, technology and semiconductor ETFs, plus DRAM and EWY by performance, holdings, investment use case, and key risks.
seo.knowledgeEventMythTitle: Market Data Myths: World Cup, Elections, Presidential Cycle, Olympics — GlobalAssetHistory
seo.knowledgeEventMythDescription: Use historical market data to test popular market myths around the World Cup, US elections, presidential cycles, and the Olympics.
seo.knowledgeTermsTitle: US Stock and ETF Glossary — GlobalAssetHistory
seo.knowledgeTermsDescription: A glossary for US stocks, ETFs, indexes, fees, holdings, premiums, drawdowns, volatility, and market data terms.
seo.wishesDescription: Submit and view feature suggestions and feedback for GlobalAssetHistory.
seo.etfMarketTitle: China ETF Market Quotes & Fee Comparison — GlobalAssetHistory
seo.etfMarketDescription: Track China-listed Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 ETFs with real-time quotes, market cap, fee totals, premiums, and 30-day tracking error.
